overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved increased park and recreation impact fees, passed several ordinances including mobile food vendor licensing and e-bike regulations, and discussed ongoing concerns about the farmers market relocation to Kennedy Park. The board also adjourned the Board of Review to June 15, 2026 and made appointments to the CLIPS committee.

agenda.item: Board of Review adjournment to June 15, 2026
Board of Trustees meeting called to order and roll call
Public hearing on park and recreation impact fees (Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015)
Approval of minutes from previous meetings
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions with chair updates
Consent agenda including vouchers and license renewals
Ordinances including mobile food vendor, nuisances, e-bikes, and transportation funding resolution
Committee appointments and farmers market discussion

discussions.body: Staff presented proposed fee increases from $300 to $761 for single family units, $250 to $563 for duplex units, and $250 to $495 for multifamily units, based on the 2022 public facilities needs assessment. Jim Pitel spoke during public comment questioning the lack of detailed operational cost data justifying the fees. Trustee Barb supported the fee increase citing population growth and fair cost sharing for new residents. Ordinance 26-010 was approved with motion by Lope Sorrell, second by Zagami, passing unanimously. Resolution 2026-015 was approved with motion by Hermling, second by Clark, passing unanimously.

Chief Finkey explained this ordinance creates a regional inspection agreement with Marshfield, Wisconsin Rapids, Stevens Point, Clover Belt, and other municipalities. When any participating fire department inspects a food truck and places a sticker, other communities will accept that inspection rather than requiring separate inspections for each municipality. Ordinance 26-008 was approved with motion by Lope, second by Zagami, passing unanimously.

The board debated amendments to section 1.08 of the elected and appointed village officials handbook. Discussion centered on removing language requiring specific justifications (health, safety, welfare) for remote attendance. Trustees Beck and others argued the policy should simply require notification, camera use, and exclusion from closed sessions without chairs having to evaluate excuses. An amendment to strike sentences requiring justification passed with Trustees Hermling and Motti voting no. The amended policy then passed with the same two dissenting votes.

The president appointed Stephanie Daniels (currently on Finance and HR) and Golden Mua to the CLIPS committee. Golden Mua expressed interest in Finance committee but will start with CLIPS. Motion by Hermling, second by Lopes, approved unanimously. The board discussed having committee application resumes included in future packets for transparency.

discussions.item: Farmers Market Location at Kennedy Park
discussions.body: Discussion-only item addressing the relocation from the former Camp Phillips/Schofield Avenue location after property owner Ray Lockery developed that site. Trustee Beck raised safety concerns about cars parking on grass along Alderson and in the park. Renee explained historical context: original location had 25 stalls, Camp Phillips location had 42 stalls without vehicle parking, and Kennedy Park now has 53 spots with vendor vehicle parking. She noted they receive approximately $5,000 in grants for SNAP/EBT token programs and the market operates without financial loss. No action was taken; discussion will continue.

Schofield Coin and Hobby LLC at 5703 Jamar Street requested secondhand article and jewelry dealer licenses. Chief Hunt explained these involve precious metals transactions with significant dollar amounts requiring tracking for stolen merchandise prevention. The license was approved with an amendment requiring satisfactory background check completion and receipt of occupancy permit, as the building is still under construction. Passed unanimously.

Jim Pitel criticized during public comment the estimated $108,000 cost for two outside engineering consultants, arguing the village should fill the vacant engineering technician position instead. Public Works Director Michael Dolan later explained the position has been advertised multiple times but the specific skillset is scarce in central Wisconsin, with even consulting firms struggling to find local candidates.

publicComment: Jim Pitel of 52nd Street spoke twice. During general public comment, he criticized the $108,000 expenditure for outside engineering consultants and questioned why the engineering technician position remains unfilled, demanding administrator accountability. He also objected to Lisa Beck being introduced as a Safer board member before official appointment, called for all committee discussions to occur publicly, and complained about lack of Kennedy Park funding information in the packet. During the impact fee hearing, he questioned the lack of detailed operational cost data for parks maintenance to justify the fee increases.
actionItems: Park and recreation impact fees increased effective with ordinance 26-010 and resolution 2026-015
Board of Review adjourned to June 15, 2026
Mobile food vendor regional inspection agreement ordinance 26-008 adopted
Remote meeting attendance policy amended to remove justification requirements
Stephanie Daniels and Golden Mua appointed to CLIPS committee
Schofield Coin and Hobby LLC secondhand dealer license approved pending background check and occupancy permit
Staff to prepare intergovernmental service agreement for building inspections assistance to Town of Ringle
Comprehensive Plan steering committee meeting scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 6:00 PM
Speed limit study on Weston Avenue to be brought back to the board
Committee application resumes to be included in future board packets
title: Board of Trustees
